ZEITZ MUST BE USED ONLY FOR PURPOSES OF THIS CASE AND DISTRIBUTED ONLY TO PERSONS WORKING ON THE CASE WITH A NEED TO KNOW THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THOSE RECORDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sonya Smallets, Esq., from Lawless & Lawless, 415-391-7555, for Plaintiff and moving party
Aaron Scolari, Esq., from Kaufman, Dolowich & Voluck & Gonzo LLP, 415-402-0059, for Defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF KIM RYDEN'S Notice Of Motion To Quash The Subpoena To Mark Oscherwitz, M.D. is granted. The subpoena is too broad and violates Plaintiff's right to privacy. Order signed in open Court.
Parties to meet and confer to try to resolve other issues on the subpoena. Judge: Marla Miller, not Reported.

Ntc Of Motion In Support Of Pltfs Motion To Quash The Subpoenas
Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHLEEN M. HURLY OF KAUFMAN DOLOWICH & GONZO LLP, TEL. NO. 403-0005 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - PLAINTIFF KIM RYDEN'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS TO JOHN A. ZEITZ AND QUALYS, INCORPORATED - Motion To Quash The Subpoenas is granted as to Qualys and denied as to Dr. Zeitz. The records sought from Dr. Zeitz are directly relevant to plaintiff's claims, while those sought from Qualys are not. The records obtained from Dr. Zeitz must be used only for purposes of this case and distributed only to persons working on the case with a need to know the information contained in those records. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)
